project_title: Comparative Genocide Lab - comparative analysis of mass political violence across ten canonical dimensions
product_name: Comparative Genocide Lab - comparative analysis of mass political violence across ten canonical dimensions
final_object_type: analytic method or human-AI evaluation module
runtime_target: structured public page plus project-specific data packet and proof runner
operational_end_state: A user can apply a method, compare baseline and method-supported reasoning, record human judgment, and preserve the claim boundary.
full_build_definition: An instrument with input, rule or rubric, analyst action, scoring or classification output, replay log, falsifier, and evidence-bound acceptance criteria.
minimum_operational_release: A usable instrument for Comparative Genocide Lab - comparative analysis of mass political violence across ten canonical dimensions: sample input, analytic rule, analyst or model action, output, scoring or review gate, and falsification test.
required_modules_to_build: baseline answer | structured review | human delta | confidence gate
source_status: 6 source-application rows; 0 active passage contracts; 0 unlocated source candidates.
source_actions_before_claim: Carry 6 source-application role(s) into the first build release and convert the most load-bearing source into a close-read contract before any quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim.
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | human-AI delta test: structured human review changes, narrows, blocks, or justifies output
acceptance_tests: Run the instrument on a sample input; verify rule application, output trace, scoring/review decision, falsifier, and claim ceiling.
promotion_stage: prototype-to-full-build; source and capture checks still govern release language
current_status: source-backed proof build; needs source-to-output capture and close-read strengthening
launch_or_review_path: Open the instrument contract for `genocide-lab`, run the sample input through the rule/rubric, and capture the output and falsifier.
first_full_build: Add a compact source-to-judgment example: input source, analytic instrument, inference made, rival interpretation, confidence limit, and downstream use.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
public_claim_now: May claim a prototype-to-full-build path exists; do not claim completed operational system.
blocking_gates: live capture and outside-use evidence before deployment or validation claims
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
